data.title: Trust Wallet, owned by Binance's CZ, to cover $7 million in user losses from a security breach. Users urged to upgrade.
data.description: Trust Wallet, a popular cryptocurrency wallet linked to Binance founder Changpeng "CZ" Zhao, has disclosed a security breach in a specific version of its browser extension. The breach resulted in the theft of at least $7 million, prompting the company to announce full reimbursement for impacted users. The issue was first noted by blockchain investigators who observed suspicious activity on the Trust Wallet browser extension during the holiday season. Following the installation of version 2.68, numerous user accounts were emptied of funds. Trust Wallet has advised users to disable the compromised extension and upgrade to the latest version, 2.69, to ensure security. Mobile users and other extension versions were not affected by the breach. For more details, visit a corresponding news platform.

data.title: Justin Wolfers criticizes media's focus on S&P 500 highs as "economic illiteracy," highlights global market surge of 30% compared to US market's 18%. Annual U.S. stock market underperformance against global peers by 12% noted. Wolfers warns of distorted view of domestic economic health without global context.
data.description: University of Michigan professor Justin Wolfers criticizes media coverage of the S&P 500 hitting record highs, calling it "economic illiteracy." Despite U.S. markets growing by 18%, global markets excluding the U.S. have surged by 30% over the past year. Wolfers argues that Americans have underperformed the global market by 12%, leading to a "miserable year for the American investor." He believes the media's focus on domestic market performance creates a distorted view of the economy. Wolfers also raises concerns about the accuracy of economic data beyond the stock market.
